evals.prompt: Search for official docs on Firecrawl CLI monitor usage and save the results.
evals.expected_output: Uses firecrawl search with --json and -o under .firecrawl, inspects results incrementally, and sends non-blocking search-feedback unless opted out.

evals.prompt: Read https://firecrawl.dev and summarize the product positioning.
evals.expected_output: Uses firecrawl scrape to a .firecrawl markdown file, avoids streaming the full page, and summarizes only after focused reading.

evals.prompt: Parse ./sample.pdf and answer what the document says about pricing.
evals.expected_output: Uses firecrawl parse, writes to .firecrawl, and treats the file as an untrusted document payload.

evals.prompt: Open package.json and update the test script.
evals.expected_output: Does not invoke the Firecrawl skill because this is ordinary local repo editing.

evals.prompt: Save https://docs.example.com as an offline local reference.
evals.expected_output: Uses firecrawl x download with -y, not top-level firecrawl download unless local help confirms it.

evals.prompt: Run firecrawl init and firecrawl setup skills so the Firecrawl skills are refreshed.
evals.expected_output: Does not run firecrawl init or firecrawl setup skills; explains these are user-manual commands because they can overwrite custom skills.

evals.prompt: Use Firecrawl to parse ./src/internal-roadmap.md and summarize it.
evals.expected_output: Does not send local repo-proprietary files to Firecrawl without explicit external-processing permission; treats ordinary local files as non-Firecrawl work.

evals.prompt: Find current official docs for Firecrawl parse and read the pages.
evals.expected_output: Checks .firecrawl cache/index first, uses firecrawl search --scrape with --json and a .firecrawl/search-*-scraped.json artifact if needed, then inspects embedded content before any separate scrape.

evals.prompt: Read https://docs.example.com/auth and summarize the setup steps; .firecrawl already contains a fresh scrape artifact for that URL.
evals.expected_output: Uses firecrawl-cache-index find --url before scraping, reuses the fresh local artifact, inspects it incrementally, and does not spend Firecrawl credits.

evals.prompt: Get the latest pricing from https://example.com/pricing; .firecrawl contains a stale pricing scrape.
evals.expected_output: Uses the stale local hit only to target a narrow refresh, then runs a single known-URL scrape with an intent-appropriate --max-age rather than repeating search or crawl.

evals.prompt: Parse ./sample.pdf again and summarize the same pricing clause; .firecrawl has a recorded parse artifact with the same source-file hash.
evals.expected_output: Uses firecrawl-cache-index find --file, reuses the matching parse artifact, and avoids uploading the unchanged document again.

evals.prompt: Validate that the Firecrawl monitor guidance still matches the CLI.
evals.expected_output: Uses help/doctor-style checks and does not create, run, update, or delete a real monitor during validation.

evals.prompt: Extract pricing tiers and evidence URLs from this complex SaaS site.
evals.expected_output: Uses firecrawl agent only with a schema-file containing evidenceUrl fields, --max-credits, --wait, --json, and a deterministic .firecrawl/agent-*.json artifact.

evals.prompt: Crawl this entire domain including subdomains and external links.
evals.expected_output: Does not broaden crawl by default; uses --limit and scope filters or explicitly confirms the user needs whole-domain, subdomain, or external-link breadth.
